news.title: Welcome to our new Summer Undergraduate Researchers!
news.excerpt: The Salguero Lab welcomes several new undergraduate research assistants joining us for the summer session.
news.content: We are excited to welcome new undergraduate chemistry students who will be working on synthesis and characterization projects. They will be paired with senior graduate student mentors to learn advanced solid-state synthesis techniques and density gradient centrifugation sorting.

news.title: Casey Rowe wins UGA Outstanding Ph.D. Dissertation Award
news.excerpt: Ph.D. graduate Casey Rowe has been awarded the UGA Chemistry Department's Outstanding Research Award.
news.content: Congratulations to Casey Rowe on receiving the Outstanding Graduate Student Research Award for her pioneering work on high-yield nanosheet exfoliation and thermally driven nanowire transformations. The award recognizes research excellence, publication records, and mentorship within the department.

news.title: Thermally Driven Nanowire Transformation Published in ACS Nano
news.excerpt: Our latest paper detailing the real-time thermal evolution of TaSe3 nanoribbons is now live in ACS Nano.
news.content: In collaboration with Oak Ridge National Lab, we used in situ heating scanning transmission electron microscopy (STEM) to capture the phase transformation of quasi-1D nanowires. This work opens new routes to custom mixed-dimensional microelectronics.

news.title: UGA Graduate Student Wins 2025 CSGS Master's Thesis Award
news.excerpt: Xena Mansoura, advised by Dr. Tina T. Salguero, received the prestigious 2025 CSGS Master's Thesis Award in Math, Physical Sciences, and Engineering.
news.content: The University of Georgia Graduate School announced that Xena Mansoura has been selected as a recipient of the 2025 Council of Graduate Schools (CSGS) Master's Thesis Award in the Math, Physical Sciences, and Engineering category. Mansoura completed her M.S. in Chemistry under the mentorship of Dr. Tina T. Salguero. Her award-winning thesis, titled 'Synthesis, Characterization, and Application of Copper and Manganese Based Luminescent Materials,' investigates novel synthetic pathways and optical behavior in functional inorganic chalcogenides and oxides. Mansoura currently serves as a Research Associate at Pacific Northwest National Laboratory (PNNL).

news.title: Salguero Lab Presents CDW Switching at Device Research Conference
news.excerpt: The group presented our work on electronic noise and threshold switching in Weyl semimetal devices in Denver, Colorado.
news.content: Graduate student Clayton Mantz delivered a talk detailing the electrical gating of 1T-TaS2 and noise scaling at the annual Device Research Conference, generating collaboration interests from industrial microelectronic partners.

news.title: New NSF Grant Awarded for Nanosheet Sorting Research
news.excerpt: The National Science Foundation (NSF) DMR program has awarded the Salguero Lab $450,000 for sorting 2D sheets.
news.content: This new three-year grant DMR-1919942 will fund the exploration of liquid-phase sorting methods, density gradient centrifugation limits, and the robotic assembly of artificial superlattices.

news.title: Local High School Outreach: Exploring Nanoscience of Ancient Pigments
news.excerpt: The Salguero Group hosted local high school chemistry students for a hands-on lab on ancient pigments.
news.content: Students synthesized Egyptian blue parent crystals and witnessed liquid exfoliation into near-infrared luminescent nanosheets, demonstrating how historical materials science connects to modern quantum nanotechnology.
